title: Burst Soy Butter Tomatoes with Eggs & Rice
summary: Sweet, jammy tomatoes in buttery soy sauce piled onto warm rice, finished with sesame-crusted fried eggs. Big flavour, low effort, proper joy.

method.description: Peel the ginger and finely mince. If using, pick the coriander leaves, discarding the stems and thinly slice the spring onions. Add the herbs into a bowl of ice cold water to crisp.

Melt the butter in a frying pan over medium heat. Add the ginger, fry for 30 secs until fragrant, then tip the tomatoes in. Cook for 4-5 mins, until they’re about to burst. Next, pour in the soy sauce, water and bring to the boil, stirring until it gets all emulsified and glossy. Then remove from the heat.
Reheat the rice. Divide between bowls and top with the soy butter tomatoes.

Place the empty pan back onto medium-high heat with a drizzle of oil. Once hot, add the sesame seeds and crack the eggs in. Cook, basting the eggs with the hot oil, until they are done to your liking.
Top the tomatoes with a sesame fried egg and some crisp herbs and the spring onions.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
